channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: ICE detention deaths are on a record pace. One Texas facility bears the brunt
item.description: ICE inspectors in February found 49 violations to detention standards at Camp East Montana, including failure from staff to"accurately document required checks to prevent significant self-harm and suicide."
item.content:encoded:
ICE inspectors in February found 49 violations to detention standards at Camp East Montana, including failure from staff to"accurately document required checks to prevent significant self-harm and suicide."

(Image credit: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Happy 125th birthday to the PB&J!
item.description: What's more accessible, reliable and universally beloved than a peanut butter and jelly sandwich? Turns out this affordable finger-food staple got its start at high tea.
item.content:encoded: What's more accessible, reliable and universally beloved than a peanut butter and jelly sandwich? Turns out this affordable finger-food staple got its start at high tea.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Shea

item.title: A botanist searches for the seeds of the rare Death Valley Sage
item.description: For more than 15 years, botanist Naomi Fraga has been trying to collect seeds from the rare Death Valley sage, for safekeeping in a vault of native California seeds.
item.content:encoded:
For more than 15 years, botanist Naomi Fraga has been trying to collect seeds from the rare Death Valley sage, for safekeeping in a vault of native California seeds.

(Image credit: Krystal Ramirez for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Christopher Intagliata

item.title: Trump administration sues three states over attempts to regulate prediction markets
item.description: The suits are the most ambitious effort to date that the Trump administration has gone to try to override state laws and set the rules for the fast-growing and increasingly divisive betting industry.
item.content:encoded:
The suits are the most ambitious effort to date that the Trump administration has gone to try to override state laws and set the rules for the fast-growing and increasingly divisive betting industry.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Attorney General Pam Bondi out at DOJ
item.description: Bondi's departure comes amid simmering frustration over her leadership and handling of the Epstein files. President Trump says Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche will be acting attorney general.
item.content:encoded:
Bondi's departure comes amid simmering frustration over her leadership and handling of the Epstein files. President Trump says Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche will be acting attorney general.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Ziggy Stardust and Hacky Sack: What life was like the last time we went to the moon
item.description: The Artemis II mission is the first time humans have headed to the moon since 1972. That year also marked the debut of The Godfather and the Egg McMuffin.
item.content:encoded:
The Artemis II mission is the first time humans have headed to the moon since 1972. That year also marked the debut of The Godfather and the Egg McMuffin.

(Image credit: Evening Standard/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Retired rear admiral talks about Trump's address on the Iran war
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks retired Rear Admiral and former national security communications adviser John Kirby about President Trump's address on the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks retired Rear Admiral and former national security communications adviser John Kirby about President Trump's address on the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: In address to the nation, Trump says war in Iran is 'nearing completion'
item.description: President Trump addressed the nation Wednesday night to outline his objectives for the war in Iran, discuss the military's progress to date and announce the war there is "nearing completion."
item.content:encoded: President Trump addressed the nation Wednesday night to outline his objectives for the war in Iran, discuss the military's progress to date and announce the war there is "nearing completion."
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Hegseth ousts the Army chief of staff as the Iran war rages through Week 5
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire, a U.S. official confirmed to NPR.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire, a U.S. official confirmed to NPR.

(Image credit: Oliver Contreras/AFP)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: As hoops betting spikes, it's New Hampshire and other states vs. prediction markets
item.description: In New Hampshire and states with legalized sports gambling, wagering helps fund government services. But now competitors like Kalshi and Polymarket are getting a cut of the action.
item.content:encoded:
In New Hampshire and states with legalized sports gambling, wagering helps fund government services. But now competitors like Kalshi and Polymarket are getting a cut of the action.

(Image credit: Zoey Knox)
item.dc:creator: Todd Bookman
